title: spiranthes romanzoffiana var. stricta
intro: Spiranthes romanzoffiana var. stricta, also known as the white lady's tresses, is a rare and unique species of orchid native to the western coast of North America. The medicinal properties of this plant have been attributed to its ability to act as an antispasmodic, relieving muscle spasms and cramps, as well as an expectorant, helping to clear congestion from the lungs. The health benefits of consuming white lady's tresses include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, and its antispasmodic properties have been used to treat menstrual cramps and other digestive issues. Herbal preparations of white lady's tresses can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
